Medium. This is a real owner-only authorization bypass, but the demonstrated impact is limited to persistent mutation of the current session’s delivery policy rather than

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: OpenClaw: `browser.request` still allows `POST /reset-profile` through the `operator.write` surface
description:
> Fixed in OpenClaw 2026.3.24, the current shipping release.

# Title

`browser.request` still allows `POST /reset-profile` through the `operator.write` surface in OpenClaw `v2026.3.22` after `GHSA-vmhq-cqm9-6p7q`

## Severity Assessment

High

CWE:

- `CWE-863: Incorrect Authorization`

Proposed CVSS v3.1:

- `8.1` (`CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H`)

An authenticated caller who only has access to the scoped Gateway method `browser.request` on the `operator.write` surface can still

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an authenticated user with limited permissions to reset user profiles via unauthorized POST requests, potentially leading to data loss, service disruption, or unauthorized configuration changes.
recommended_actions: Immediately update OpenClaw to version 2026.3.24 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Audit and restrict access to the `operator.write` surface to minimize exposure to unauthorized users.
Implement monitoring and logging for suspicious activity related to the `browser.request` method to detect potential exploitation.

title: OpenClaw: Mutating internal `/allowlist` chat commands missed `operator.admin` scope enforcement
description:
> Fixed in OpenClaw 2026.3.24, the current shipping release.

**Title**  
Mutating internal `/allowlist` chat commands missed `operator.admin` scope enforcement

**CWE**  
CWE-862 Missing Authorization

**CVSS v3.1**  
CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N  
Base score: **6.5 (Medium)**

**Severity Assessment**  
Medium. This is a real authorization flaw in OpenClaw’s internal control plane. The issue does not require host access, trusted local state tampering, or multi-tenant assumptions

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in OpenClaw allows unauthorized users with limited privileges to mutate internal `/allowlist` chat commands without proper enforcement of the `operator.admin` scope. This could lead to unauthorized modifications to access control lists, potentially enabling privilege escalation or unauthorized access.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to OpenClaw version 2026.3.24 or later, as the issue is resolved in this release.
Conduct an audit of `/allowlist` usage and access logs to identify any unauthorized modifications prior to patching.
Implement additional monitoring and alerting for sensitive commands like `/allowlist` to detect anomalous activity.

title: OpenClaw: Gateway operator.write Can Reach Admin-Class Channel Allowlist Persistence via chat.send
description:
> Fixed in OpenClaw 2026.3.24, the current shipping release.

## Summary

The shared `/allowlist` command persists channel authorization config through `writeConfigFile(...)` but does not re-validate gateway client scopes for internal gateway callers. Because `chat.send` is intentionally reachable to `operator.write` callers and still creates a generic command-authorized internal context, an authenticated write-scoped gateway client can indirectly mutate channel `allowFrom` and `groupAllowFrom`

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: OpenClaw has a Gateway HTTP /v1/models Route Bypasses Operator Read Scope
description:
> Fixed in OpenClaw 2026.3.24, the current shipping release.

## Summary

The OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint `/v1/models` accepts bearer auth but does not enforce operator method scopes.

In contrast, the WebSocket RPC path enforces `operator.read` for `models.list`.

A caller connected with `operator.approvals` (no read scope) is rejected for `models.list` (`missing scope: operator.read`) but can still enumerate model metadata through HTTP `/v1/models`.

Confirmed on current `main` at commit `

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: OpenClaw has incomplete Fix for CVE-2026-32011: Feishu Webhook Pre-Auth Body Parsing DoS (Slow-Body / Slowloris Variant)
description:
> Fixed in OpenClaw 2026.3.24, the current shipping release.

# Advisory Details

**Title**: Incomplete Fix for CVE-2026-32011: Feishu Webhook Pre-Auth Body Parsing DoS (Slow-Body / Slowloris Variant)

**Description**:

### Summary

The patch for CVE-2026-32011 tightened pre-auth body parsing limits (from 1MB/30s to 64KB/5s) across several webhook handlers. However, the **Feishu extension's webhook handler** was not included in the patch and still accepts request bodies with the old permissive l

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The incomplete fix for CVE-2026-32011 leaves the Feishu webhook handler vulnerable to a Slow-Body/Slowloris-style Denial of Service (DoS) attack. This could allow attackers to exhaust server resources by sending slow, incomplete HTTP requests, potentially impacting availability of services relying on the Feishu webhook integration.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply rate-limiting and connection timeout policies at the web server or API gateway level to mitigate Slow-Body/Slowloris attacks.
Contact the OpenClaw development team to confirm when a complete fix will be released and monitor for updates.
Conduct a security audit of all webhook handlers, particularly those integrated with Feishu, to identify and mitigate similar vulnerabilities.

title: OpenAirInterface V2.2.0 AMF crashes when it fails to decode the message. Not all decode failures...
description: OpenAirInterface V2.2.0 AMF crashes when it fails to decode the message. Not all decode failures result in a crash. But the crash is consistent for particular inputs. An example input in hex stream is 80 00 00 0E 00 00 01 00 0F 80 02 02 40 00 58 00 01 88.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in OpenAirInterface V2.2.0 AMF allows for a crash when decoding specific malformed messages. This could lead to denial-of-service (DoS) attacks, disrupting critical network functions in environments relying on OpenAirInterface for 5G core network implementations.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest patched version of OpenAirInterface V2.2.0 or apply any available security patches addressing CVE-2026-30077.
Implement input validation and sanitization mechanisms to detect and reject malformed messages before processing.
Monitor network traffic for anomalous patterns that could indicate exploitation attempts and enable logging to identify potential attack vectors.

title: The iconv() function in the GNU C Library versions 2.43 and earlier may crash due to an assertion...
description:
The iconv() function in the GNU C Library versions 2.43 and earlier may crash due to an assertion failure when converting inputs from the IBM1390 or IBM1399 character sets, which may be used to remotely crash an application.



This vulnerability can be trivially mitigated by removing the IBM1390 and IBM1399 character sets from systems that do not need them.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the iconv() function of the GNU C Library could allow remote attackers to crash applications by exploiting an assertion failure when converting inputs from specific character sets (IBM1390 or IBM1399). This may lead to denial-of-service (DoS) conditions in affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately assess whether IBM1390 and IBM1399 character sets are required on your systems. If not, remove them to mitigate the risk.
Apply patches or updates to the GNU C Library as soon as they are available to address CVE-2026-4046.
Implement monitoring and logging to detect unusual crashes or behavior in applications using the iconv() function.

title: A cross-session information disclosure vulnerability exists in the awesome-llm-apps project in...

The affected Streamlit-based GitHub MCP Agent stores user-supplied API tokens in process-wide environment variables using os.environ without proper session isolation. Because Streamlit serves multiple concurrent users from a single Python process, credentials provided by one user remain accessible to subsequent unauthenticated users. An atta

title: A vulnerability was detected in raine consult-llm-mcp up to 2.5.3. Affected by this vulnerability...
description: A vulnerability was detected in raine consult-llm-mcp up to 2.5.3. Affected by this vulnerability is the function child_process.execSync of the file src/server.ts. The manipulation of the argument git_diff.base_ref/git_diff.files results in os command injection. The attack is only possible with local access. The exploit is now public and may be used. Upgrading to version 2.5.4 addresses this issue. The patch is identified as 4abf297b34e5e8a9cb364b35f52c5f0ca1d599d3. Upgrading the affected compon

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for OS command injection via the child_process.execSync function in src/server.ts, potentially enabling attackers with local access to execute arbitrary commands. This could lead to unauthorized system modifications, data exfiltration, or further exploitation of the system.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to version 2.5.4 of raine consult-llm-mcp to apply the patch identified as 4abf297b34e5e8a9cb364b35f52c5f0ca1d599d3.
Restrict local access to trusted users only and monitor for any unusual activity in systems running the vulnerable software.
Conduct a security audit to identify any potential exploitation of this vulnerability and ensure all systems are updated.

title: Smoothwall Express versions prior to 3.1 Update 13 contain a reflected cross-site scripting...
description: Smoothwall Express versions prior to 3.1 Update 13 contain a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability in the /redirect.cgi endpoint due to improper sanitation of the url parameter. Attackers can craft malicious URLs with javascript: schemes that execute arbitrary JavaScript in victims' browsers when clicked through the unsanitized link.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in osrg GoBGP up to 4.3.0. Affected is the function...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in osrg GoBGP up to 4.3.0. Affected is the function BGPHeader.DecodeFromBytes of the file pkg/packet/bgp/bgp.go of the component BGP Header Handler. The manipulation leads to improper access controls. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The attack is considered to have high complexity. The exploitability is told to be difficult. The identifier of the patch is f0f24a2a901cbf159260698211ab15c583ced131. To fix this issue, it is recommended to de

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the BGP Header Handler of osrg GoBGP up to version 4.3.0 could allow improper access controls, potentially enabling unauthorized access or manipulation of BGP routing information. While the exploit is considered complex and difficult to execute, successful exploitation could disrupt network routing and lead to data interception or denial of service.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest patched version of osrg GoBGP that includes the fix (commit ID: f0f24a2a901cbf159260698211ab15c583ced131).
Conduct a thorough review of network configurations and access controls to ensure proper security measures are in place.
Monitor network traffic for unusual or unauthorized BGP activity and implement intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A weakness has been identified in osrg GoBGP up to 4.3.0. This impacts the function...
description: A weakness has been identified in osrg GoBGP up to 4.3.0. This impacts the function DecodeFromBytes of the file pkg/packet/bgp/bgp.go. Executing a manipulation of the argument data[1] can lead to off-by-one. The attack may be launched remotely. Attacks of this nature are highly complex. The exploitability is said to be difficult. This patch is called 67c059413470df64bc20801c46f64058e88f800f. A patch should be applied to remediate this issue.

title: A user with access to the cluster with a limited set of privilege actions can trigger a crash of...
description:
A user with access to the cluster with a limited set of privilege actions can trigger a crash of a mongod process during the limited and unpredictable window when the cluster is being promoted from a replica set to a sharded cluster. This may cause a denial of service by taking down the primary of the replica set.

This issue affects MongoDB Server v8.2 versions prior to 8.2.2, MongoDB Server v8.0 versions between 8.0.18, MongoDB Server v7.0 versions between 7.0.31.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5170.

description: MRCMS V3.1.2 contains an unauthenticated directory enumeration vulnerability in the file management module. The /admin/file/list.do endpoint lacks authentication controls and proper input validation, allowing remote attackers to enumerate directory contents on the server without any credentials.

title: Smoothwall Express versions prior to 3.1 Update 13 contain a stored cross-site scripting...
description: Smoothwall Express versions prior to 3.1 Update 13 contain a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in the /cgi-bin/vpnmain.cgi script due to improper sanitation of the VPN_IP parameter. Authenticated attackers can inject arbitrary JavaScript through VPN configuration settings that executes when the affected page is viewed by other users.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability allows authenticated attackers to inject malicious JavaScript into VPN configuration settings. This could lead to session hijacking, data theft, or unauthorized actions performed on behalf of other users who view the affected page.
recommended_actions: Update Smoothwall Express to version 3.1 Update 13 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Review and sanitize user inputs in the VPN configuration settings to prevent injection attacks.
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to mitigate the impact of potential XSS attacks.

title: In KubePlus 4.1.4, the mutating webhook and kubeconfiggenerator components have an SSRF...
description: In KubePlus 4.1.4, the mutating webhook and kubeconfiggenerator components have an SSRF vulnerability when processing the chartURL field of ResourceComposition resources. The field is only URL-encoded without validating the target address. More critically, when kubeconfiggenerator uses wget to download charts, the chartURL is directly concatenated into the command, allowing attackers to inject wget's `--header` option to achieve arbitrary HTTP header injection.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SSRF vulnerability in KubePlus 4.1.4 allows attackers to manipulate the chartURL field, potentially leading to unauthorized access to internal systems, data exfiltration, and arbitrary HTTP header injection via wget commands. This poses a significant risk to environments using Kubernetes for resource composition and management.
recommended_actions: Immediately update KubePlus to a patched version if available, or apply mitigations such as disabling the vulnerable components.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for the chartURL field to prevent malicious payloads.
Restrict network access to internal resources and monitor for unusual activity originating from Kubernetes components.

title: A Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in SourceCodester Sales and Inventory...
description: A Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in SourceCodester Sales and Inventory System 1.0. The application fails to sanitize the input, allowing remote attackers to inject arbitrary web script or HTML via a crafted URL.

title: CVE-2025-10679 - The ReviewX – WooCommerce Product Reviews with Multi-Criteria, Reminder Emails, Google Reviews, Sche
description: The ReviewX – WooCommerce Product Reviews with Multi-Criteria, Reminder Emails, Google Reviews, Schema & More plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary method calls in all versions up to, and including, 2.2.12. This is due to insufficient input validation in the bulkTenReviews function that allows user-controlled data to be passed directly to a variable function call mechanism. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to call arbitrary PHP class methods that take no inputs or have default values, potentially leading to information disclosure or remote code execution depending on available methods and server configuration.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-10679.

description: A security flaw has been discovered in SourceCodester Sales and Inventory System 1.0. title: CVE-2026-4606 - GV Edge Recording Manager (ERM) v2.3.1 improperly runs application components with SYSTEM-level priv
description:
GV Edge Recording Manager (ERM) v2.3.1 improperly runs application components with SYSTEM-level privileges, allowing any local user to gain full control of the operating system. 

During installation, ERM creates a Windows service that runs under the LocalSystem account. 

When the ERM application is launched, related processes are spawned under SYSTEM privileges rather than the security context of the logged-in user. 

Functions such as 'Import Data' open a Windows file dialog operating with SYSTEM permissions, enabling modification or deletion of protected system files and directories. 

Any ERM function invoking Windows file open/save dialogs exposes the same risk. 

This vulnerability allows local privilege escalation and may result in full system compromise.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4606.

title: CVE-2026-2580 - The WP Maps – Store Locator,Google Maps,OpenStreetMap,Mapbox,Listing,Directory & Filters plugin for
description: The WP Maps – Store Locator,Google Maps,OpenStreetMap,Mapbox,Listing,Directory & Filters plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to time-based SQL Injection via the ‘orderby’ parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.9.1 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query.
